body.en: A mortgage finances an asset that may appreciate. A payday loan finances a shortfall at 400% APR. Both are debt, but they operate on entirely different mechanics. Recognising which type of debt you are dealing with is the first step to evaluating whether it makes your financial situation better or worse.

title.en: Secured vs unsecured debt
title.ru: Обеспеченный и необеспеченный долг

body.en: Secured debt is backed by collateral — an asset the lender can seize if you default. Mortgages (collateral: property) and auto loans (collateral: vehicle) are secured. Because the lender has recourse, rates are lower. Unsecured debt has no collateral — credit cards, personal loans, microloans. The lender bears more risk, so rates are higher. Defaulting on secured debt means losing the asset; defaulting on unsecured triggers collections and credit damage.
body.ru: Русский перевод: Secured debt is backed by collateral — an asset the lender can seize if you default. Mortgages (collateral: property) and auto loans (collateral: vehicle) are secured. Because the lender has recourse, rates are lower. Unsecured debt has no collateral — credit cards, personal loans, microloans. The lender bears more risk, so rates are higher. Defaulting on secured debt means losing the asset; defaulting on unsecured triggers collections and credit damage.
body.kk: Қазақша аударма: Secured debt is backed by collateral — an asset the lender can seize if you default. Mortgages (collateral: property) and auto loans (collateral: vehicle) are secured. Because the lender has recourse, rates are lower. Unsecured debt has no collateral — credit cards, personal loans, microloans. The lender bears more risk, so rates are higher. Defaulting on secured debt means losing the asset; defaulting on unsecured triggers collections and credit damage.

example.en: Mortgage at 12% (secured by flat). Personal loan at 24% (unsecured). Microloan at 120%+ APR (unsecured, short-term). The rate difference directly reflects the lender's collateral protection — or lack of it.

title.en: Instalment vs revolving debt
title.ru: Долг с рассрочкой и возобновляемый долг

body.en: Instalment debt has a fixed schedule: borrow once, repay in equal payments over a set term. Mortgages, car loans, and student loans work this way — the balance falls predictably. Revolving debt has a limit you can borrow and repay repeatedly. Credit cards are the main example. The danger: revolving debt has no automatic endpoint. You can carry a balance indefinitely, paying interest forever without reducing principal if you only pay the minimum.
body.ru: Русский перевод: Instalment debt has a fixed schedule: borrow once, repay in equal payments over a set term. Mortgages, car loans, and student loans work this way — the balance falls predictably. Revolving debt has a limit you can borrow and repay repeatedly. Credit cards are the main example. The danger: revolving debt has no automatic endpoint. You can carry a balance indefinitely, paying interest forever without reducing principal if you only pay the minimum.

Mortgages, car loans, and student loans work this way — the balance falls predictably. Revolving debt has a limit you can borrow and repay repeatedly. Credit cards are the main example. The danger: revolving debt has no automatic endpoint. You can carry a balance indefinitely, paying interest forever without reducing principal if you only pay the minimum.

title.en: How Interest Works
title.ru: Как работают проценты

description.en: Interest is not a flat fee — it compounds on unpaid balances. Understanding the mechanics reveals why small debts become large ones.
description.ru: Русский перевод: Interest is not a flat fee — it compounds on unpaid balances. Understanding the mechanics reveals why small debts become large ones.
description.kk: Қазақша аударма: Interest is not a flat fee — it compounds on unpaid balances. Understanding the mechanics reveals why small debts become large ones.

title.en: You are not paying interest on what you borrowed
title.ru: Вы платите проценты не только на то, что взяли взаймы
title.kk: Сіз пайызды тек алған сомаңызға ғана төлемейсіз

body.en: Most people think of interest as a fixed fee on the original loan amount. In reality, interest is calculated on the outstanding balance — which grows if you don't pay enough each period. Miss a payment or pay only the minimum, and next month's interest is charged on a higher base. This is how a manageable debt becomes an unmanageable one.
body.ru: Русский перевод: Most people think of interest as a fixed fee on the original loan amount. In reality, interest is calculated on the outstanding balance — which grows if you don't pay enough each period. Miss a payment or pay only the minimum, and next month's interest is charged on a higher base. This is how a manageable debt becomes an unmanageable one.
body.kk: Қазақша аударма: Most people think of interest as a fixed fee on the original loan amount. In reality, interest is calculated on the outstanding balance — which grows if you don't pay enough each period. Miss a payment or pay only the minimum, and next month's interest is charged on a higher base. This is how a manageable debt becomes an unmanageable one.

tip.en: Every unpaid tenge of interest becomes principal. Next month, interest is charged on that too.
tip.ru: Русский перевод: Every unpaid tenge of interest becomes principal. Next month, interest is charged on that too.
tip.kk: Қазақша аударма: Every unpaid tenge of interest becomes principal. Next month, interest is charged on that too.

title.en: Simple vs compound interest on debt
title.ru: Простые и сложные проценты по долгу

body.en: Simple interest charges a fixed percentage of the original principal each period — rare in consumer lending. Compound interest charges a percentage of the current outstanding balance, which includes previously unpaid interest. Most credit cards, consumer loans, and microloans use compound interest. This means unpaid interest is added to the balance and itself earns interest — the same compounding that builds investment wealth works against you in debt.
body.ru: Русский перевод: Simple interest charges a fixed percentage of the original principal each period — rare in consumer lending. Compound interest charges a percentage of the current outstanding balance, which includes previously unpaid interest. Most credit cards, consumer loans, and microloans use compound interest. This means unpaid interest is added to the balance and itself earns interest — the same compounding that builds investment wealth works against you in debt.
body.kk: Қазақша аударма: Simple interest charges a fixed percentage of the original principal each period — rare in consumer lending. Compound interest charges a percentage of the current outstanding balance, which includes previously unpaid interest. Most credit cards, consumer loans, and microloans use compound interest. This means unpaid interest is added to the balance and itself earns interest — the same compounding that builds investment wealth works against you in debt.

example.en: Borrow 100,000 ₸ at 3% monthly. Month 1: 3,000 ₸ interest. If unpaid, Month 2 interest is on 103,000 ₸ = 3,090 ₸. Month 3 on 106,090 ₸ = 3,183 ₸. After 12 months without any payment: balance = ~142,576 ₸. The debt grew by 42.6% in one year.

title.en: How loan amortisation works
title.ru: Как работает амортизация кредита

body.en: On an instalment loan, each payment covers two things: accrued interest and a reduction of principal. In the early months, most of the payment goes to interest because the outstanding balance is large. As the principal shrinks, the interest portion falls and more of each payment chips away at the balance. This front-loading of interest is why paying off a loan early saves disproportionately — you eliminate the most interest-heavy period.

As the principal shrinks, the interest portion falls and more of each payment chips away at the balance. This front-loading of interest is why paying off a loan early saves disproportionately — you eliminate the most interest-heavy period.

This front-loading of interest is why paying off a loan early saves disproportionately — you eliminate the most interest-heavy period.

tip.en: On a 5-year loan, extra payments in years 1–2 save far more interest than the same payments in years 4–5, because you cut the high-interest early period.

title.en: What happens when you miss a payment?
title.ru: Что происходит, если пропустить платёж?

en.instruction: A borrower has a 500,000 ₸ balance at 2% monthly interest. Evaluate what happens under each payment scenario after 3 months.
options: Balance falls
Balance unchanged
Balance grows

scenarios.text: The borrower pays exactly 10,000 ₸ per month. Monthly interest accrues at 2% = 10,000 ₸ on the 500,000 ₸ balance.
scenarios.explanation: Paying exactly the interest keeps the balance flat but makes zero progress on principal. The borrower is running in place — paying indefinitely and never reducing the debt.

scenarios.text: The borrower misses one month entirely, then resumes normal minimum payments the following month.
scenarios.explanation: The missed month's interest (10,000 ₸) is added to principal. Now interest is charged on 510,000 ₸. One missed payment permanently increases the balance and every future interest charge.

scenarios.text: The borrower pays 25,000 ₸ per month: 10,000 ₸ covers interest, 15,000 ₸ reduces principal.
scenarios.explanation: Any payment above the interest charge reduces principal. The smaller the balance, the less interest accrues — creating a virtuous cycle that accelerates payoff.

title.en: Interest is compounding working against you
title.ru: Проценты — это капитализация, работающая против вас
title.kk: Пайыз — сізге қарсы жұмыс істейтін күрделі өсу

body.en: The same mechanics that make investing powerful — returns on returns — make debt dangerous when unpaid. Every missed payment or minimum-only payment adds to the principal that earns next month's interest. APR is the standardised way to compare these rates across different products and compounding frequencies, which is why it matters.
body.ru: Русский перевод: The same mechanics that make investing powerful — returns on returns — make debt dangerous when unpaid. Every missed payment or minimum-only payment adds to the principal that earns next month's interest. APR is the standardised way to compare these rates across different products and compounding frequencies, which is why it matters.

Every missed payment or minimum-only payment adds to the principal that earns next month's interest. APR is the standardised way to compare these rates across different products and compounding frequencies, which is why it matters.

tip.en: On any debt, calculate: how much of my monthly payment goes to interest? That's the cost of delay. Any extra payment goes directly to principal and reduces all future interest.

title.en: Lenders quote rates in ways that flatter them
title.ru: Кредиторы называют ставки так, чтобы они выглядели выгоднее
title.kk: Кредиторлар мөлшерлемені өздеріне тиімді көрінетіндей көрсетеді

body.en: A lender might advertise '3% per month' or '2% processing fee plus 18% annual rate' or '0% interest — just a small service charge.' These all describe borrowing costs, but they cannot be directly compared without a common standard. Annual Percentage Rate (APR) converts any borrowing cost into a standardised annual figure, making comparison possible.

These all describe borrowing costs, but they cannot be directly compared without a common standard. Annual Percentage Rate (APR) converts any borrowing cost into a standardised annual figure, making comparison possible.

Annual Percentage Rate (APR) converts any borrowing cost into a standardised annual figure, making comparison possible.

tip.en: Always convert any quoted rate to APR before comparing. '3% per month' is 36% APR — not 3%.

title.en: What APR includes — and what it doesn't
title.ru: Что включает APR — и что не включает
title.kk: APR нені қамтиды және нені қамтымайды

body.en: APR captures the interest rate plus mandatory fees (origination fees, insurance required by the lender, administrative charges) expressed as an annual percentage of the loan amount. It does not include optional insurance, late fees, or prepayment penalties — those add to the true cost but are excluded from the APR calculation. A loan with a low APR but heavy late fees can cost more in practice than a higher-APR loan repaid on time.

It does not include optional insurance, late fees, or prepayment penalties — those add to the true cost but are excluded from the APR calculation. A loan with a low APR but heavy late fees can cost more in practice than a higher-APR loan repaid on time.

A loan with a low APR but heavy late fees can cost more in practice than a higher-APR loan repaid on time.

title.en: Total Cost of Credit: the number that really matters
title.ru: Полная стоимость кредита: число, которое действительно важно
title.kk: Кредиттің толық құны: шын мәнінде маңызды сан

body.en: APR tells you the rate. Total Cost of Credit (TCC) tells you the absolute tenge amount you will pay above the principal. TCC = all repayments − original loan amount. On a long-term loan, TCC can exceed the principal — you pay back more than twice what you borrowed. This figure should be front of mind for any major borrowing decision, not just the monthly payment.

On a long-term loan, TCC can exceed the principal — you pay back more than twice what you borrowed. This figure should be front of mind for any major borrowing decision, not just the monthly payment.

title.en: Which loan is actually cheaper?
title.ru: Какой кредит на самом деле дешевле?

en.instruction: Each borrower is comparing two loan offers. Find the reasoning error in their conclusion.

cases.text: "Loan A charges 2% per month. Loan B charges 26% per year. I'll take Loan B — 26% sounds higher than 2%."
options: a) No error — 26% annual is higher than 2% monthly
b) Error — 2% monthly = 24% APR, so Loan B at 26% APR is actually more expensive
cases.explanation: 2% monthly compounds to approximately 26.8% effective annual rate. But even as simple APR, 2% × 12 = 24% vs Loan B's 26%. Comparing monthly to annual rates without conversion leads to the wrong choice.

cases.text: "Both loans are 500,000 ₸ at 18% APR. Loan A is 2 years; Loan B is 4 years. I'll take Loan B — lower monthly payment means it's cheaper."
options: a) No error — lower monthly payment always means lower total cost
b) Error — lower monthly payment comes from a longer term, which increases total interest paid
cases.explanation: Same APR, longer term = more months of interest. Loan B's lower monthly payment is offset by paying for twice as long. Total cost of Loan B is substantially higher than Loan A.

cases.text: "This loan has a 0% interest rate for 12 months, then 28% APR. I'll pay it off in 10 months to avoid the high rate."
options: a) No error — paying off before the rate kicks in avoids the 28% APR
b) Error — you cannot pay off a 0% loan early without penalty
cases.explanation: Using a 0% promotional period and paying off before it expires is a legitimate strategy. As long as the full balance is cleared before month 12, no high-rate interest is incurred. The plan is sound.

en.final_explanation: APR standardises rate comparison. Term length determines total cost. Always calculate total repayment = monthly payment × number of payments, then subtract the principal to find true interest cost.

body.en: APR is the right tool for comparing two loans at the same term. Total Cost of Credit is the right tool for understanding what borrowing actually costs you in tenge. Use both: APR to filter options, TCC to make the final call. This framework becomes critical when evaluating the most dangerous products in the KZ market: microloans and payday loans.

title.en: Fast money, compound catastrophe
title.ru: Быстрые деньги, сложная катастрофа

body.en: Microfinance organisations (MFOs) in Kazakhstan offer loans approved in minutes with no credit check, often framed as a convenient solution for short-term cash gaps. The true cost is buried in a daily rate that converts to 200–600% APR. For millions of Kazakhstani borrowers, these products are the entry point to a debt spiral that takes years to exit.

title.en: How the debt trap forms: rollover and reborrowing
title.ru: Как возникает долговая ловушка: продление и новые займы
title.kk: Қарыз тұзағы қалай пайда болады: ұзарту және қайта қарыз алу

body.en: A payday loan is typically due in full in 7–30 days. The borrower, unable to repay in full, 'rolls over' the loan — extending the term by paying only the fee, resetting the clock. Or they take a new loan to repay the old one. Each rollover adds fees to principal. Within a few cycles, the total owed can exceed the original sum. The trap is not dishonesty — it is arithmetic combined with tight cash flow.
body.ru: Русский перевод: A payday loan is typically due in full in 7–30 days. The borrower, unable to repay in full, 'rolls over' the loan — extending the term by paying only the fee, resetting the clock. Or they take a new loan to repay the old one. Each rollover adds fees to principal. Within a few cycles, the total owed can exceed the original sum. The trap is not dishonesty — it is arithmetic combined with tight cash flow.

Each rollover adds fees to principal. Within a few cycles, the total owed can exceed the original sum. The trap is not dishonesty — it is arithmetic combined with tight cash flow.

example.en: Borrow 50,000 ₸ for 30 days at 1.5%/day. Day 30 interest: 22,500 ₸. Total due: 72,500 ₸. Unable to pay in full, borrower rolls over for another 30 days — now 72,500 ₸ × 1.5%/day compounds. After 90 days without full repayment: balance ≈ 153,000 ₸ on an original 50,000 ₸ loan.

title.en: Regulatory context and legal protections in Kazakhstan
title.ru: Регулирование и правовая защита в Казахстане

body.en: The National Bank of Kazakhstan has introduced limits on MFO rates, and the total debt owed to an MFO cannot legally exceed the original principal by more than a set multiplier. Despite this, enforcement is uneven and many borderline-legal products exist. The most important protection is not regulatory — it is refusing to borrow from MFOs for non-emergency consumption needs. An emergency fund (Topic 4) is the real alternative.

title.en: Exit strategy: which option breaks the cycle?
title.ru: Стратегия выхода: какой вариант разрывает цикл?
title.kk: Шығу стратегиясы: қай нұсқа циклді бұзады?

en.instruction: Sanzhar has an MFO debt of 200,000 ₸ at 0.9%/day (~329% APR). He has several options. Which one actually breaks the debt cycle versus which merely delays or worsens it?
options: Breaks the debt cycle
Delays or worsens the cycle

scenarios.text: Sanzhar takes a new 200,000 ₸ MFO loan from a different lender to fully repay the first one.
scenarios.explanation: Replacing one high-rate MFO debt with another resets the clock without reducing the cost. He still owes the same amount at the same rate — and may now have fees from the new loan too.

scenarios.text: Sanzhar borrows 200,000 ₸ from a family member at 0% interest and repays the MFO immediately, then repays the family member over 6 months.
scenarios.explanation: Replacing the MFO debt with a 0% informal loan stops the compounding immediately. Even a formal personal bank loan at 25% APR would break the cycle — anything with a lower rate than ~329% APR wins.

scenarios.text: Sanzhar continues making minimum MFO payments while waiting for his annual bonus to pay off the full balance.
scenarios.explanation: At 0.9%/day, minimum payments almost certainly cover less than the daily interest accrual. Each month of waiting adds thousands of tenge to the balance. Unless the bonus arrives within days, the debt will be larger when he finally pays.

title.en: Prevention is the only reliable defence
title.ru: Профилактика — единственная надёжная защита

body.en: The exit from MFO debt requires replacing it with a lower-rate instrument: a bank personal loan, a family loan, or an employer advance. Any of these dominates the MFO rate and stops the compounding. But the most powerful tool is not taking the loan in the first place — which requires a functional emergency fund, a skill from earlier sections. Credit history is what opens the door to lower-rate alternatives over time.

description.en: Your credit history is a financial track record that determines your access to lower-rate borrowing. In Kazakhstan, the First Credit Bureau (FCB) tracks it — knowing how it works lets you build and protect it.

title.en: Your borrowing past determines your future rate
title.ru: Ваш прошлый опыт займов определяет вашу будущую ставку
title.kk: Бұрынғы қарыз тарихыңыз болашақ мөлшерлемеңізді анықтайды

body.en: Lenders have no way to observe your financial behaviour directly. Credit history is the record they use instead — a standardised track record of how reliably you have repaid past obligations. A strong credit history unlocks lower rates, higher limits, and faster approvals. A poor one means higher costs or outright rejection, pushing you toward the high-rate lenders of last resort.

title.en: What goes into your credit history
title.ru: Что входит в кредитную историю

body.en: KZ credit bureaus record: every loan and credit product you have opened, payment dates (on time or late), outstanding balances, number of credit enquiries (each application is logged), and any defaults or restructurings. Payment history is the most heavily weighted factor — a single 60-day late payment can damage your score for years. Enquiries matter less but accumulate quickly if you shop for credit frequently.

example.en: Behaviour that builds credit: on-time payments every month, low utilisation on credit cards, long relationship with one or two lenders, rare credit applications. Behaviour that damages it: missed payments, defaults, using 90%+ of credit card limits, multiple loan applications in a short period.

title.en: How to build credit from zero in Kazakhstan
title.ru: Как построить кредитную историю с нуля в Казахстане
title.kk: Қазақстанда кредит тарихын нөлден қалай қалыптастыруға болады

body.en: With no credit history, lenders treat you as an unknown risk. The fastest way to build history is a secured credit card (deposit-backed) or a small instalment loan that you repay on time for 6–12 months. Each on-time payment is reported to the bureau and improves your file. Starting small and reliable is better than starting large — the goal is a track record, not a large credit line.

title.en: Credit history is a long-term asset
title.ru: Кредитная история — долгосрочный актив

body.en: A strong credit file is built over years and damaged in weeks. It is not a score to chase for its own sake — it is infrastructure that determines your access to lower-cost borrowing for homes, cars, and business. The discipline that builds credit (on-time payments, low utilisation) is the same discipline that prevents debt problems. The final piece is knowing which debts to pay off first and in what order.

tip.en: If you have a poor credit history, the path back is slow and mechanical: pay on time, every time, for 12–24 consecutive months. There is no shortcut.

title.en: Debt Payoff Strategies
title.ru: Стратегии погашения долгов

description.en: Two proven methods — avalanche and snowball — approach the same goal differently. Choosing the right one depends on whether you optimise for mathematics or psychology.

title.en: More than minimum: the only way out
title.ru: Больше минимума: единственный выход
title.kk: Минимумнан көп төлеу: жалғыз шығу жолы

body.en: Minimum payments on multiple debts keep you in debt indefinitely while maximising total interest paid. Getting out requires making more than the minimums — and directing the extra money strategically. Two systematic methods exist: the avalanche (mathematical optimum) and the snowball (psychological optimum). Both work; the best one is the one you will actually follow.

Getting out requires making more than the minimums — and directing the extra money strategically. title.en: The debt avalanche: highest rate first
title.ru: Долговая лавина: сначала самая высокая ставка
title.kk: Қарыз лавинасы: алдымен ең жоғары мөлшерлеме

body.en: The avalanche method targets the highest-interest debt with all extra money, while paying minimums on everything else. When that debt is cleared, the freed payment rolls into the next highest-rate debt — creating an accelerating payoff. Mathematically, this minimises total interest paid and clears debt fastest in tenge terms. The drawback: high-rate debts are often the largest, so the first win can take a long time.

When that debt is cleared, the freed payment rolls into the next highest-rate debt — creating an accelerating payoff. Mathematically, this minimises total interest paid and clears debt fastest in tenge terms. The drawback: high-rate debts are often the largest, so the first win can take a long time.

example.en: Debt A: 50,000 ₸ at 36% (MFO). Debt B: 300,000 ₸ at 22% (credit card). Debt C: 1,000,000 ₸ at 15% (personal loan). Avalanche order: A → B → C. Even though A is small, its 36% rate means every day it exists costs more per tenge than either other debt.

title.en: The debt snowball: smallest balance first
title.ru: Долговой снежный ком: сначала самый маленький остаток
title.kk: Қарыз қарлы добы: алдымен ең кіші қалдық

body.en: The snowball method targets the smallest balance first, regardless of interest rate. Each cleared debt delivers a concrete win and frees up a payment to add to the next debt. Psychologically, the early victories sustain motivation through what is otherwise a long, invisible process. The cost: you may pay more total interest if small debts have lower rates. Research shows borrowers using the snowball method are more likely to stay the course and become debt-free.

tip.en: Choose avalanche if you are highly analytical and motivated by data. Choose snowball if you have struggled to maintain financial habits in the past — the wins matter more than the math.

blocks.text: Three debts, 50,000 ₸/month available for payoff (above minimums combined). Which method clears debt faster and at lower total cost?

In this case the order happens to be the same for both methods (smallest balance also has the highest rate). The avalanche saves ~9,000 ₸. In scenarios where they diverge, the avalanche saves more but the snowball delivers earlier wins on smaller debts. The difference is rarely enormous — consistency matters more than method selection.

title.en: The method matters less than the commitment

body.en: Both avalanche and snowball outperform the alternative — making minimum payments and hoping the debt eventually disappears. The real enemy of debt payoff is not the wrong method; it is the absence of any method. Pick one, automate the extra payments, and do not accumulate new high-rate debt while paying off the old. With the debt management framework complete, you now have the full toolkit for the Debt & Credit topic.
